A Record of
Lives Well Lived

Linden Cemetery has served the families of Atchison County since the mid-1800s. This database was compiled from original ownership deeds, hand-drawn plot maps, headstone audits, and records held by the Atchison County Historical Society.

Whether you are tracing family history, conducting genealogical research, or simply honoring those at rest here, we hope this archive serves you well.

This History

About
Linden Cemetery

Linden Cemetery is a historic rural cemetery located in Atchison County, Missouri, serving the community since the mid-nineteenth century. The grounds contain hundreds of plots ranging from the earliest pioneer settlers of the region to residents of the present day.

This database was created to provide permanent, searchable access to interment records that were previously scattered across handwritten ledgers, ownership deeds, hand-drawn plot maps, and the institutional memory of local historical societies.

Cemetery History

c. 1840s
Earliest documented interments in the Linden area, as pioneer families settled Atchison County and established community burial grounds.
1847
Cyrus O. and Amelia Porter among the first to have formal plot assignments recorded — their monument remains standing in the North section today.
Late 1800s
The cemetery expanded significantly as the county's population grew. Veterans of the Civil War were interred, and the first formal plot ownership deeds were issued.
1975
A formal plot ownership map was created, providing the first systematic spatial record of all North section burial locations. This map forms the basis of the interactive plot viewer in this database.
Present
The Linden Cemetery database compiles records from multiple historical sources into a single searchable archive, now available to researchers and families worldwide.

Sources & Acknowledgments

This database draws on records from several institutions and individuals whose work made this archive possible:

Atchison County Historical Society
Primary source for biographical annotations and genealogical notes
Linden Cemetery Association
Plot maps, ownership deeds, and burial records
Field Audit Teams
Headstone photography and on-site grave marker verification
Community Volunteers
Transcription, data entry, and genealogical research

About This Database

The database currently contains 1,351 interment records spanning from the 1840s to the present, including 83 veterans, across 447 documented plots in the North and South sections. Records include names, dates, birth and death locations, biographical notes, grave marker information, and — for a growing number of entries — photographs of headstones taken in the field.

If you have information to contribute — corrections, additions, photographs, or family histories — please use the Contact page. All submissions are reviewed before being incorporated into the archive.

Contact
Us

Have a correction, a photograph to contribute, or a question about a record? We welcome all correspondence from researchers, genealogists, and families.

For Record Corrections
Use the form to describe the correction, including the plot number if known. We review all submissions carefully before updating the archive.
For Photo Submissions
High-resolution JPEG photos of grave markers are especially welcome. Please include the plot and space number in your message.
For Genealogical Inquiries
We can assist with family research to the best of our records. Response times vary based on volunteer availability.
